issue_ids: FORMULA_PATTERN_ANOMALY|Revenue|C4|Formula pattern differs from neighboring copied formulas in the same row/column run.|8cd6cf29

impact_factors.explanation: Copied formula family has at least five neighboring cells, so a local anomaly affects a wide block.

audit_findings.issue_id: FORMULA_PATTERN_ANOMALY|Revenue|C4|Formula pattern differs from neighboring copied formulas in the same row/column run.|8cd6cf29
audit_findings.message: Formula pattern differs from neighboring copied formulas in the same row/column run.
audit_findings.rationale: A formula in a copied row/column run does not match the normalized pattern used by its immediate neighbors, which often signals a paste error or one-off adjustment.
audit_findings.remediation: Compare the outlier cell with its neighboring formulas, confirm whether the difference is intentional, and align the formula with the copied pattern when it is not.
audit_findings.title: Formula pattern anomaly

impact_factors.explanation: Control or assumption values are embedded directly in a formula instead of labeled inputs.

audit_findings.rationale: Numeric literals embedded in formulas are harder to audit, reuse, and update than values stored in labeled input cells.
audit_findings.remediation: Move constants into clearly labeled input cells or named ranges and reference those cells from the formula.
audit_findings.title: Hardcoded numeric constant
